description_long: This object consists of a cluster of four garlic bulbs, each with a faceted, polygonal shape and a pale beige color. The bulbs are attached together at the top by short, thick, grayish stems, which are bundled and secured with a square band. The garlic bulbs display subtle ridges and pointed root tufts at their bases, giving them a natural, organic appearance. The papery skin is visible, covering the bulbs with a matte, slightly rough texture. The cloves within each bulb are suggested by the segmented surface geometry, though they remain tightly packed and not fully separated. The overall form is compact and realistic, suitable for placement in urban market scenes or kitchen environments.
description: A cluster of four pale, polygonal garlic bulbs with visible root tufts and bundled gray stems, covered in papery skin.
description_view_0: Front view showing four garlic bulbs clustered together, with stems bundled at the top and root tufts visible at the bottom.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the vertical alignment of the bulbs and the thickness of the bundled stems.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the segmentation of the bulbs and the pointed root tufts more prominently.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the broad surfaces of the bulbs and the attachment of the stems.
attribute_bulb_segmentation: The bulbs are whole with visible segmentation lines suggesting individual cloves, but the cloves are not fully separated. Four main segments are visible, with moderate clarity.
attribute_clove_arrangement: The cloves are tightly packed within each bulb, arranged in a roughly radial pattern around the central stem, showing some symmetry and layering.
attribute_root_tuft_visibility: visible, with small, pointed, dark brown tufts at the base of each bulb
attribute_stem_remnant: present, with short, thick, grayish stems bundled at the top and secured by a square band
